codeReview.summary: The requested fix is not implemented. There is no worker transcript or handoff for `beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix`, no new fix commit to review, and the repository still contains the original blocking behaviors from `beta-command-webhooks`: rerun/focus idempotency keys are time-based rather than tied to a stable note delivery identifier, and note-hook persistence remains outside an atomic/deduplicated command-execution flow.

issues.description: `computeCommandIdempotencyKey` still salts rerun/focus command keys with `time.Now().UnixNano()`. The same note delivery retried after a transient failure therefore produces a fresh idempotency key and can create duplicate rerun/focus side effects instead of at-most-once behavior keyed to a stable note delivery identifier.

`handleNoteEvent` still inserts the `hook_events` row before command execution and does so outside a transaction shared with command side effects. If command execution fails after that insert, a GitLab retry of the same delivery is later deduplicated by the existing hook row and the requested rerun/ignore/resolve/focus effect is permanently lost.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `go-integration` skill does not tell workers how to proceed when a follow-up fix feature has no concrete worker session, handoff, or commit to review. Reviewers had to infer from mission state that the original failing implementation is still current.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` shows `beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix` with `completedWorkerSessionId: null`, the worker transcript JSONL has no entry for that feature, and the only available handoff/commit for this area is the original `beta-command-webhooks` commit `29ae864468dfe743886317306885fd4242d72954`.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed `beta-command-webhooks` handoff/diff, checked mission state for the requested fix feature, and inspected the current command/webhook implementation. The fix review fails because no fix session or diff exists and the original blocking idempotency/atomicity defects are still present in `internal/commands/commands.go` and `internal/hooks/handler.go`.
